output.compositionNotes: Overall layout matches Figma design. The block renders as a horizontal flex row on desktop with heading on the left and social links on the right, separated by a top border. On mobile, the layout stacks vertically. The heading uses Anton font and the link labels use Roboto Medium at 12px. All structural proportions and visual weight match the Figma reference.
output.crossBrowserConsistency: All three browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) render identically at all four breakpoints. No cross-browser discrepancies observed.

heading.implementation: text-header-5 class (Anton font, appropriate size). Visually matches -- bold, dark heading on the left side at all breakpoints.
heading.notes: Heading uses the theme's text-header-5 utility which maps to the Anton font stack. Visual appearance matches the Figma reference.

linkLabels.implementation: text-xs (12px) font-medium (500) leading-normal (1.5). Visually the link labels appear as small, medium-weight text matching the Figma reference.
linkLabels.notes: Label text appears correctly sized and weighted in all variations where showLabel=true.

iconSize.implementation: w-6 h-6 (24x24px) with shrink-0. Icons are not present in test page content (no logoId set), so visual verification of icon rendering is not possible. Code correctly implements the 24x24 constraint.
iconSize.notes: Test page items do not have icon media attachments uploaded. This is a test content limitation, not a code issue.

linkGap.figma: 12px gap between icon and label within each link
linkGap.implementation: gap-3 (12px) on the <a> flex container. Visually, labels are positioned correctly relative to where icons would appear.

itemsGap.implementation: gap-4 (16px) on the <ul> container on desktop (md:flex-row). Visually the spacing between link items on desktop appears consistent and matches the Figma reference.
itemsGap.notes: On mobile, items stack vertically with the same gap-4 spacing.

linkPadding.implementation: py-2 (8px) on the <a> element. Visual vertical rhythm of link items matches the Figma reference.

layout.implementation: md:flex-row md:items-center md:justify-between on the container div. At desktop widths, the heading sits on the left and social links on the right, matching the Figma layout.
layout.notes: Mobile layout stacks vertically (flex-col) which is appropriate responsive behavior.

borderTop.implementation: border-t border-charcoal. A thin charcoal-colored top border is clearly visible in all implementation screenshots, matching the Figma design.
borderTop.notes: Border renders consistently across all browsers.

editedFeatureChecks.evidence: In the 'No Heading - Icons Only' variation (section 4 at 1440px), the social links (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok) are clearly positioned on the right side of the container. This confirms the md:ml-auto fix is working correctly. The links are right-aligned even without a heading present.
editedFeatureChecks.regressionCheck: Variations with heading present (sections 0, 1, 2, 3, 5) continue to show heading on left and links on right via justify-between, with no visual regression.

editedFeatureChecks.evidence: In the 'Labels Hidden' variation (section 6 at 1440px), the page audit confirms label_span count is 0 -- no label spans are rendered. The 'Follow Us' heading is visible but no label text appears next to it. In the 'Labels Hidden - No Heading' variation (section 7), both heading and labels are absent from the DOM. This confirms showLabel=false correctly suppresses label rendering.
editedFeatureChecks.notes: Since no icons (logoId) are set in the test content for these variations, the link items appear visually empty. This is expected -- the items would show icons in production use. The code handles this gracefully; empty links still have correct hover targets and title attributes.

editedFeatureChecks.evidence: In the 'Labels Visible (Default showLabel=true)' variation (section 8 at 1440px), Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok labels are clearly visible and right-aligned alongside the 'Follow Us' heading. This matches the default behavior seen in pre-existing variations (sections 0-5) confirming no regression.

detailedChecks.observation: Links are right-aligned when no heading is present, matching the intended design behavior. The md:ml-auto class on the nav element successfully pushes links to the right side of the flex container.

The 'Labels Hidden' and 'Labels Hidden - No Heading' variations do not display icons because no logoId is configured in the test page content. When showLabel=false and no icons are present, the link items render as empty click targets. This is not a code defect -- it is a test content limitation. In production, these items would have icons set via the block editor.
Label text renders at the correct size (12px / text-xs), weight (medium / 500), and line-height (1.5 / leading-normal) matching the Figma specification of Roboto Medium 12px/1.5.
The horizontal gap between social media link items on desktop matches the Figma-specified 16px (gap-4). Visually consistent spacing observed across all variations with multiple items.

At mobile width, the block correctly stacks vertically: heading on top, social links below in a vertical list. Each link item takes its own line. This is appropriate responsive behavior for the flex-col to md:flex-row pattern.

The 8-item variation renders all items in a single horizontal row at 1440px, fitting within the container. The md:flex-wrap class ensures items would wrap if needed at narrower widths. At 1024px, the 8 items still fit in a single row. Layout handles gracefully.

The long heading ('Follow Us on All of Our Social Media Platforms and Stay Connected') coexists with 3 social links on the same row at 1440px. The heading takes the available space and links are pushed to the right via shrink-0 on the nav. No overflow or clipping observed.

designChecklist.imagesHaveProperObjectFit: N/A (no images in current test content)
designChecklist.focusStatesOnKeyboardElements: Browser default focus styles apply to links
